Directions (68): In each of the questions below some statements are given followed by two conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts.
-
Q6. Statements: 
All flowers are plants.
Only a few plants are fruits.
No fruits is vegetables.
Conclusion I: Some flowers are fruits.
Conclusion II: Some plants are not vegetables.
q:
Directions (68): In each of the questions below some statements are given followed by two conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts.
-
Q6. Statements: 
All flowers are plants.
Only a few plants are fruits.
No fruits is vegetables.
Conclusion I: Some flowers are fruits.
Conclusion II: Some plants are not vegetables.

options.text: If either conclusion I or II follows
If neither conclusion I nor II follows
If both conclusions I and II follow

explanation: I: No relation is guaranteed between flowers and fruits (False). II: The part of plants that is fruits cannot be vegetables since no fruits is vegetables (True). Only II follows.

q:

Q7. Statements: 
All mammals are bird.
All birds are eggs.
No eggs are reptiles.
Conclusion I: Some mammals are eggs.
Conclusion II: Some reptiles can be birds.

options.text: If either conclusion I or II follows
If neither conclusion I nor II follows
If both conclusions I and II follow

explanation: I: All mammals are birds, and all birds are eggs, so all mammals are eggs, which implies some are eggs (True). II: Since no eggs are reptiles, no birds (which are inside eggs) can be reptiles (False). Only I follows.

question: In the number '479486553', if odd digits are subtracted by 1 and even digits are subtracted by 2. Then, what will be the sum of the digits which are 2nd, 4th and 6th from the left end in the new number formed after rearrangement?
q: In the number '479486553', if odd digits are subtracted by 1 and even digits are subtracted by 2. Then, what will be the sum of the digits which are 2nd, 4th and 6th from the left end in the new number formed after rearrangement?
explanation: $Original: 479486553. Applying operations: even2 (4>2, 4>2, 8>6, 6>4), odd1 (7>6, 9>8, 5>4, 5>4, 3>2). New number: 268264442. 2nd digit=6, 4th=2, 6th=4. Sum = 12$.

question: The average weight of eight people is X kg. Two new people joined them with total weight of 151 kg and average weight of all people increased by 1.5 kg (\frac{3}{2} kg). If the weight of lightest people out of two people joined is X5 kg, then find the difference between weight of these two people who joined later.
q: The average weight of eight people is X kg. Two new people joined them with total weight of 151 kg and average weight of all people increased by 1.5 kg (\frac{3}{2} kg). If the weight of lightest people out of two people joined is X5 kg, then find the difference between weight of these two people who joined later.
explanation: $8X + 151 = 10(X + 1.5) ⇒ 2X = 136 ⇒ X = 68. Lightest = 63 kg. Heaviest = 151 × 63 = 88 kg. Difference = 25 kg$.

question: A gets 40% marks in an exam and he received 40 less marks than passing marks. B gets 70% marks in same exam and received 20 more marks than passing marks. If C gets 65% marks in the same exam, then find the difference between marks received by A and C in the exam.
q: A gets 40% marks in an exam and he received 40 less marks than passing marks. B gets 70% marks in same exam and received 20 more marks than passing marks. If C gets 65% marks in the same exam, then find the difference between marks received by A and C in the exam.
explanation: $Let total marks = 100y. 40y + 40 = 70y 20 ⇒ 30y = 60 ⇒ y = 2. Total = 200. A = 80, C = 130. Difference = 50$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: In a mixture, the quantity of milk is x liters and water are 8 liters. Five liters pure milk and six liters of water added in the mixture so that milk becomes 75% of resultant mixture. If 10 liters of resultant mixture is removed, then find the remaining quantity of mixture (in liters).
q: In a mixture, the quantity of milk is x liters and water are 8 liters. Five liters pure milk and six liters of water added in the mixture so that milk becomes 75% of resultant mixture. If 10 liters of resultant mixture is removed, then find the remaining quantity of mixture (in liters).
explanation: $(x+5)/14 = \frac{3}{1} ⇒ x = 37. Resultant total = 56. After removing 10L: remaining = 46 liters$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: In a rightangled triangle hypotenuse is 8\sqrt{10} cm and based is half of the height. If height and based of the triangle are equal to height -&- radius of a cylinder, then find curved surface area of cylinder.
q: In a rightangled triangle hypotenuse is 8\sqrt{10} cm and based is half of the height. If height and based of the triangle are equal to height -&- radius of a cylinder, then find curved surface area of cylinder.
explanation: $h^2 + 4h^2 = 640 ⇒ 5h^2 = 640 ⇒ h = 8\sqrt{2}. Height = 16\sqrt{2}, Radius = 8\sqrt{2}. CSA = 2 pi R H = 2 pi 8\sqrt{2} 16\sqrt{2} = 512pi cm^2-$.

q: The ratio of speed of P to speed of Q is 5:8, P covers D km distance in 3 hours and Q covers (D+40) km in 2.5 hours. If P increases his speed by 25%, then find the time taken by P to cover (D+180) km distance. -(in hours)
explanation: $D = 15y, D + 40 = 20y ⇒ y = 8. Speed P = 40. D = 120. New speed P = 50. Time for 300 km = 6 hours$.

explanation: $Let multiples be 4x, 4(x+1). . . 20x + 40 = 200 ⇒ x = 8. Smallest multiple = 32$.

question: The speed of boat A in still water is 4 times the speed of stream and covers 24 km upstream in 8 hours. If the speed of boat B in still water is 2 kmph more than the speed of boat A in downstream, then find the time (in minutes) taken by boat B to cover the same distance in upstream.
q: The speed of boat A in still water is 4 times the speed of stream and covers 24 km upstream in 8 hours. If the speed of boat B in still water is 2 kmph more than the speed of boat A in downstream, then find the time (in minutes) taken by boat B to cover the same distance in upstream.
explanation: $A upstream = 3s = 3 km/h ⇒ s = 1. A downstream = 5. B still water = 7. B upstream = 6. Time = 24 ÷ 6 = 4 hours = 240 minutes$.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Average of present age A, B and C is 14 years, while four years ago, average age of B, C and D was 15 years. If the sum of present of A and D is 27 years, then find the age of D five years hence will be (in years).
q: Average of present age A, B and C is 14 years, while four years ago, average age of B, C and D was 15 years. If the sum of present of A and D is 27 years, then find the age of D five years hence will be (in years).
explanation: $A+B+C = 42. B+C+D = 57. D A = 15. D + A = 27 ⇒ 2D = 42 ⇒ D = 21. Five years hence = 26 years$.

question: A seller has 230 kg of mangoes which he purchases at rate of Rs. 10 per kg. 20% of total mangoes were rotten in packaging and thrown away. If seller sold half of the remaining mangoes at the rate of Rs. 20 per kg, then at what price (per kg) should seller sell the remaining mangoes to gain a total profit of 15% on the total quantity seller had initially (in Rs. -/kg)?
q: A seller has 230 kg of mangoes which he purchases at rate of Rs. 10 per kg. 20% of total mangoes were rotten in packaging and thrown away. If seller sold half of the remaining mangoes at the rate of Rs. 20 per kg, then at what price (per kg) should seller sell the remaining mangoes to gain a total profit of 15% on the total quantity seller had initially (in Rs. -/kg)?
explanation: $CP = 2300. Required revenue = 2645. Remaining = 184. First half revenue = 92 × 20 = 1840. Required second half revenue = 805. Price = 805 ÷ 92 = Rs 8.75$.
